title: 10 predictions for the Red Sox this season
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox's Garrett Crochet is predicted to win the Cy Young Award, following a strong 18-5 record last season.
Outfielder Roman Anthony is expected to elevate from a rising star to a legitimate superstar this season.
Boston Red Sox projected to finish with a 92-70 record, reaching the ALDS after winning the wild card series.

title: Developer vows to present more ‘options’ for Davis Square tower project amid neighborhood opposition
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Developer Copper Mill plans to propose new options for a 26-story tower in Davis Square, responding to neighborhood opposition.
Copper Mill's proposal includes 502 apartments with 126 affordable units, but critics argue it doesn't match Davis Square's character.
The project may temporarily impact local businesses like The Burren and McKinnon’s Meat Market, raising concerns among residents.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte, 37, from Somerville, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his nephews, Sebastian and Zachary.
Both nephews were diagnosed with Down syndrome; Sebastian has passed away, and Zachary continues to inspire Steven.
Ponte aims to honor Sebastian's memory and support Down syndrome awareness through the Massachusetts Down Syndrome Congress.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Iranian drone strike in Kuwait killed six U.S. soldiers and injured Boston firefighter Mark Garcia.
Mark Garcia, also an Army National Guard Major, is recovering in Germany following the attack.
Garcia, affiliated with Boston's Engine 50 in Charlestown, was praised by Local 718 union for his dedication.

title: Lowell murder trial delayed after lead State Police investigator allegedly causes fatal crash while driving drunk
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lowell murder trial delayed due to allegations against lead investigator, Sgt. Scott Quigley, in a fatal crash.
Quigley allegedly drove drunk, causing 2023 Woburn crash fatality; his .11 BAC complicates trial proceedings.
Judge cites fair trial concerns; trial for Phan brothers postponed pending investigation into Quigley's conduct.

title: ​Bruins’ playoff hopes might rest on Jeremy Swayman’s shoulders for this stretch run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman is crucial to the Bruins' playoff bid, holding a .906 save percentage over 42 games.
Bruins are in the second Wild Card spot in the East with a 36-22-6 record, needing key wins for playoffs.
With struggles from Joonas Korpisalo, Swayman is expected to play a larger role in Bruins' final games.

title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston police released body cam footage showing Patriots players partying at Estella restaurant on January 27.
The event included unauthorized adult entertainment, smoking, and after-hours drinking, resulting in a license suspension for Estella.
No arrests were made, but restaurant owner Helder George Brandao faced violations and considered legal action.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Byard signs a one-year, $9 million deal with the New England Patriots.
Byard led the NFL with seven interceptions in 2025, contributing to Chicago Bears' league-best plus-22 turnover differential.
The durable safety has never missed a game in his 10-year NFL career, playing for Titans, Eagles, and Bears.

title: Man resentenced in 2011 slaying of Dorchester teen
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Nyasani Watt is resentenced to 14-17 years for the 2011 shooting of Dorchester teen Jaivon Blake.
Watt's previous murder conviction was overturned in 2024 due to a right to counsel violation.
Watt receives time served credit, making him immediately eligible for parole after current sentencing.

title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams released a new beer variety pack with themes inspired by Boston athletes White, Crochet, Edelman, and Chara.
The variety pack includes a wheat ale, pale ale, lager, and pilsner, each reflecting the athlete's style or sport.
A public launch event featuring the athletes will be held at Banners Kitchen & Tap in Boston on April 4.
